_search_results:
**Yes, the paper exists.**

- **Exact title**: A Rational Analysis of the Selection Task as Optimal Data Selection[1][3][6]
- **Authors**: Mike Oaksford and Nick Chater[1][3][6]
- **Venue**: Psychological Review[1][3][6]
- **Year**: 1994[1][3][6]
- **Volume/Issue/Pages**: Vol. 101, No. 4, pp. 608-631[1][3]
- **DOI**: Not provided in search results (PDF available via academic repositories)[1]

The paper presents a Bayesian model of optimal data selection to explain performance on Wason's selection task, arguing it reflects rational hypothesis-testing adapted to environmental rarity assumptions.[1][2][3] Later works cite and revise it.[2][4][5]
